title: If the kubelet config.yaml configuration file is being used validate file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: If the kubelet config.yaml configuration file is being used validate file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that if the kubelet refers to a configuration file with the --config argument, that file is owned by root:root.
input_row.rationale: The kubelet reads various parameters, including security settings, from a config file specified by the --config argument. If this file is specified you should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root.
input_row.audit: Automated AAC auditing has been modified to allow CIS-CAT to input a variable for the <PATH>/<FILENAME> of the kubelet config yaml file. Please set $kubelet_config_yaml=<PATH> based on the file location on your system for example: export kubelet_config_yaml=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml To perform the audit manually: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on each worker node. For example, stat -c %a /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml ```Verify that the ownership is set to `root:root`.
input_row.remediation: Run the following command (using the config file location identified in the Audit step) chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf
input_row.default_value: By default, /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml file as set up by kubeadm is owned by root:root .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each worker node where the kubelet is running.
2. Run the command: `stat -c %U:%G /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml`
3. Verify that the output shows `root:root`.
4. If the ownership is not set to `root:root`, run the command: `chown root:root /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml` to remediate the issue.
